topics.name: Basic Concepts and Political Ideologies
topics.description: Definition of government, state, nation, sovereignty, power, authority, legitimacy, democracy, capitalism, socialism.

subtopics.name: Fundamental Concepts of Government
objectives: Define government as an institution of state, process of governing, and academic field
Distinguish state, nation, society, and government
Analyze concepts of sovereignty, power, authority, legitimacy, and political culture

subtopics.name: Political Ideologies and Systems of Government
objectives: Compare capitalism, socialism, communism, communalism, fascism, and feudalism
Compare presidential system, parliamentary system, unitary system, federal system, and confederal system

topics.name: Organs of Government and Rule of Law
topics.description: Legislature, Executive, Judiciary, Separation of Powers, Checks and Balances, Rule of Law, and Citizenship.

subtopics.name: Organs of Government
objectives: Describe composition, functions, and types (unicameral, bicameral) of Legislature
Describe composition, types, and functions of Executive and Judiciary
Analyze Separation of Powers and Checks and Balances in governance

subtopics.name: Rule of Law, Constitutionalism, and Citizenship
objectives: Explain principles of Rule of Law and factors limiting Rule of Law
Describe types and characteristics of Constitutions (written, unwritten, rigid, flexible)
Explain rights, duties, and obligations of citizens

topics.name: Constitutional Development and Politics in Nigeria
topics.description: Pre-colonial political systems, colonial administration, nationalist movements, constitutional history (1922-1999), and Federalism.

subtopics.name: Pre-Colonial Administration and Colonial Rule
objectives: Compare Hausa/Fulani emirate system, Yoruba Oyo kingdom system, and Igbo decentralized political system
Analyze British Indirect Rule system in Northern, Western, and Eastern Nigeria

subtopics.name: Constitutional History and Nigerian Federalism
objectives: Analyze Clifford (1922), Richards (1946), Macpherson (1951), Lyttelton (1954), Independence (1960), Republican (1963), and 1979/1999 Constitutions
Trace development of Nigerian Federalism, state creation, and revenue allocation formulas
